item.title: Supply chain security — vetting pipeline for skills and tools
steps: Create src/security/vetting.ts for supply chain security logic
Implement skill source verification: check source against known registries before sandboxing
Implement AI-powered pattern scanning: detect suspicious patterns — outbound network calls to non-allowlisted domains, credential access, filesystem writes outside workspace
Implement VirusTotal integration for scanning skill artifacts
Implement tool allowlist management: known-safe package list with version pinning; non-allowlisted installs require explicit manual override with warning
Implement `clawhq evolve audit`: show full change history with sources, timestamps, vetting results, and rollback status
Write unit tests for vetting rules and false-positive filtering
acceptanceCriteria: Community skill install triggers source verification and AI pattern scan before user approval prompt
VirusTotal scan runs on skill artifacts as part of vetting pipeline
Non-allowlisted tool installs are blocked with risk explanation and manual override path
`clawhq evolve audit` shows vetting results alongside change history
False-positive filtering prevents blocking common benign patterns
